name: Rockefeller Banking
description: Rockefeller Banking encompasses conspiracy theories surrounding the Rockefeller family's influence on the global financial system. Proponents often claim the Rockefellers, particularly through entities like Chase Manhattan Bank (now JPMorgan Chase), have exerted undue control over monetary policy, manipulated markets, and engineered global events to consolidate their wealth and power. These theories frequently suggest a hidden agenda aimed at establishing a New World Order or a global government controlled by a financial elite. The origins of these claims can be traced back to early 20th-century criticisms of monopolies and the concentration of wealth during the Gilded Age, fueled by populist and anti-establishment sentiments. Evidence cited by proponents includes the Rockefellers' vast philanthropic activities (interpreted as a means of shaping social and political agendas), their historical involvement in central banking, and perceived connections to influential global organizations. Mainstream historical and economic perspectives generally acknowledge the significant role of the Rockefeller family and their institutions in the development of American and global finance. However, they attribute their success to factors like business acumen, innovation, and strategic investments within a competitive market. Academics often criticize conspiracy theories for lacking empirical evidence, relying on selective interpretation of historical events, and promoting unfounded accusations of malicious intent. They argue that the complex dynamics of global finance cannot be reduced to a simple narrative of control by a single family or entity, and that attributing such power to the Rockefellers oversimplifies the roles of governments, regulatory bodies, and other market participants.
subtitle: Conspiracy theories of financial control

claims.claim: The Rockefellers control the global financial system.
claims.evidence: Vast wealth, historical influence in banking, perceived control over central banks.
claims.criticism: Exaggerated influence, ignores other powerful financial institutions and regulatory bodies, lacks concrete evidence of direct control.

claims.claim: The Rockefellers manipulate markets for their own benefit.
claims.evidence: Historical market activities, alleged insider information, perceived connections to powerful individuals.
claims.criticism: Difficult to prove intent, market fluctuations are complex and influenced by numerous factors, correlation does not equal causation.

claims.claim: The Rockefellers use their wealth to promote a New World Order agenda.
claims.evidence: Philanthropic activities, involvement in global organizations, perceived support for international governance.
claims.criticism: Misinterpretation of philanthropic goals, ignores diverse perspectives within global organizations, lacks evidence of a unified, malevolent agenda.

keyFigures.name: David Rockefeller
keyFigures.role: Often cited as a key figure in alleged global control.

keyFigures.name: Gary Allen
keyFigures.role: Author of 'None Dare Call It Conspiracy', a key text for proponents.

keyFigures.name: Joseph E. Stiglitz
keyFigures.role: Nobel laureate economist critical of unchecked corporate power and financial inequality.

timeline.event: Rise of Standard Oil and the Rockefeller fortune.
Establishment of the Federal Reserve System, fueled conspiracy theories.
Globalization and the rise of international finance, intensifying conspiracy theories.

sources.author: Ron Chernow
sources.work: Titan: The Life of John D. Rockefeller, Sr.
sources.title: Ron Chernow

sources.author: Edwin Black
sources.work: Banking on Baghdad: Inside Iraq's 79-Year Relationship with the Griggs Group, J.P. Morgan, and the Modern History of American Banking
sources.title: Edwin Black
